frontmatter.title: Project Requirements
frontmatter.description: High-level goals and constraints for the OpenAuth Proxy.

content.rawMarkdown:
# Requirements

## Goals
- Provide a stateless reverse proxy for JWT validation.
- Sub-10ms latency overhead.

## Non-Goals
- Identity Provider (IdP) management (use Okta/Auth0 instead).

# Technical Overview

The proxy is written in Rust using the `hyper` crate. It sits at the edge, intercepting incoming HTTP requests, validating the `Authorization` header against a cached JWKS payload, and forwarding valid traffic to upstream microservices.

![Architecture Diagram](./assets/arch-diagram.png)

frontmatter.title: Data Model
frontmatter.description: Redis caching schema for revoked tokens.

content.rawMarkdown:
# Data Model

While the proxy is largely stateless, it maintains a connection to Redis to check for forcibly revoked tokens (JTI blocklist).

```json
{
  "key": "jti:{token_id}",
  "value": "revoked",
  "ttl": "matches_jwt_exp"
}

```

frontmatter.title: Proxy Routes
frontmatter.description: Configuration syntax for mapping external routes to internal services.

content.rawMarkdown:
# Routes Configuration

Routes are defined in a `proxy.yaml` file mounted to the container.

## Example Route

```yaml
routes:
  - path: /api/v1/users/*
    upstream: http://user-service:8080
    strip_path: true
```

frontmatter.title: Examples & Usage
frontmatter.description: How to deploy the proxy locally via Docker Compose.

content.rawMarkdown:
# Examples

## Docker Compose

To spin up the proxy alongside a mock upstream service, use the following command:

`docker-compose up -d`

Then, issue a test request with curl:

`curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://localhost:3000/api/test`
